question: Create a C++ program that prompts the user to enter a `std::string`. Then, print the entered string to the console in reverse order.
driver: int main() { std::string s; std::cout << "Enter a string: "; std::cin >> s; std::string reversed_s = s; std::reverse(reversed_s.begin(), reversed_s.end()); std::cout << "Reversed string: " << reversed_s << std::endl; return 0; }
errors: The manual string reversal logic contains a semantic error due to incorrect indexing (using 's.length() - i' instead of 's.length() - 1 - i'), which leads to an out-of-bounds access and undefined behavior when swapping characters.
